geopolitics_summary: US extends Russian oil sanctions waiver amid Strait of Hormuz closure; EU-US trade tensions persist; Middle East conflict pressures crude/energy supplies.

themes.headline: US extends Russian oil sanctions waiver into June amid supply pressure
themes.why_it_matters: Crude supply tension (Hormuz closure, extended waiver) supports oil prices; high crude feeds fuel-switching demand away from gas, supporting EU power. Direct impact on gas-to-power margin and thermal generation merit.

themes.headline: Meloni seeks energy spending flexibility carveout from EU fiscal rules
themes.why_it_matters: If approved, Italy gains fiscal room for energy subsidies/investment, potentially affecting European energy prices and competitive dynamics across power/gas markets. Signals ongoing EU energy cost pressure.

themes.headline: Far-right parties align with greens to slow EU renewables permitting liberalization
themes.why_it_matters: Delays to renewable build-out increase structural supply-tightness; slower capacity means higher baseload reliance and upward pressure on gas demand and power prices through 2026–27.

themes.headline: EU and US near trade deal; auto tariffs threat remains contingent
themes.why_it_matters: Trade uncertainty affects euro, cost of capital, and EU industrial competitiveness; weaker euro/growth headwinds could suppress demand for EU power and gas into summer.

themes.headline: US industrial natural gas consumption forecast to hit records in 2026–27
themes.why_it_matters: Record US gas demand tightens global LNG availability, reducing US export volumes to Europe (NW Europe LNG arb squeeze); supports TTF via reduced supply arbitrage and higher LNG spot pricing.

watchlist: G7 energy ministers' next meeting; further US sanctions policy announcements on Russian oil.
EU trade deal finalization vote; Trump auto tariff decision deadline.
Italy's energy spending carveout outcome in EU fiscal rule negotiations.
EU renewable permitting reform progress; far-right/green coalition impact on timelines.
